papers.title: Attention Is All You Need: Transformer Architecture Revolution
papers.abstract: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely. Experiments show these models superior in quality while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.abstract: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text.

papers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
papers.abstract: We present a residual learning framework to ease the training of networks that are substantially deeper than those used previously. We explicitly reformulate the layers as learning residual functions with reference to the layer inputs, instead of learning unreferenced functions.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Nets
papers.abstract: We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data.

papers.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
papers.abstract: We introduce Adam, an algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions, based on adaptive estimates of lower-order moments. The method is straightforward to implement, is computationally efficient, and has little memory requirements.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.abstract: We demonstrate that scaling up language models greatly improves task-agnostic, few-shot performance. GPT-3 achieves strong performance on many NLP datasets, including translation, question-answering, and cloze tasks.

papers.title: Improved Guarantees and a Multiple-Descent Curve for Column Subset Selection and the Nystrom Method
papers.abstract: The Nystrom method is a popular technique for generating low-rank approximations of kernel matrices. We analyze the method and provide novel guarantees that improve upon existing bounds.

papers.title: Vector Database Systems: A Survey and Performance Evaluation
papers.abstract: Vector databases have emerged as essential infrastructure for AI applications. This paper surveys the landscape of vector database systems, comparing architectural decisions, indexing strategies, and query performance across different implementations.

papers.title: Endee: A High-Performance Vector Database for AI Applications
papers.abstract: We present Endee, a vector database optimized for AI search and retrieval workloads. Endee supports hybrid dense-sparse retrieval, advanced filtering, and quantization strategies for billion-scale vector collections on single-node deployments.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.abstract: We explore techniques for combining parametric and non-parametric memory in language models. Our RAG models achieve state-of-the-art results on open-domain QA tasks while generating more specific, diverse, and factual responses.
